title: Belloa lehmannii
taxonomy: Belloa lehmannii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to South America. Belloa lehmannii is classified in the genus Belloa, which is a group of plants commonly known as 'marialias' or 'marijuana relatives'. The plant is further classified within the tribe Senecioneae, a group of daisy-like plants. Belloa lehmannii is closely related to other plants in the same genus, such as Belloa pinnatifida and Belloa rutilans.

The plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. intro: Belloa lehmannii, also known as Queñua de la Cordillera, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Andean region. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Belloa lehmannii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Belloa lehmannii is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in combination with other plants to create teas, tinctures, and infusions.
